title: Post-sedation follow-up documentation

examination.text: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.
examination.rationale: The exact BSG section supports recovery progress, complications, information exchange, and follow-up planning only for gastrointestinal endoscopy sedation.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.

source.title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines on sedation in gastrointestinal endoscopy

examination.text: Supports formal documented discharge assessment, recovery from sedation, absence of complications, vital and respiratory status, pain or discomfort review, procedure-appropriate instructions, contact information, and activity restrictions after sedation.
examination.rationale: The discharge section was reviewed but not mapped to a generic post-sedation workflow because it is procedure- and endoscopy-specific.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports formal documented discharge assessment, recovery from sedation, absence of complications, vital and respiratory status, pain or discomfort review, procedure-appropriate instructions, contact information, and activity restrictions after sedation.

source.title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines on sedation in gastrointestinal endoscopy

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 18–19 — suitability for discharge and instructions

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documented oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain or other recovery observations, and use of a recovery score where appropriate.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The ASA standard supports documenting recovery observations after monitored anesthesia care without generating a recovery conclusion or treatment.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documented oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain or other recovery observations, and use of a recovery score where appropriate.

source_limitations: Sedation agent and dose, procedure, airway events, baseline status, recovery interval, residual effect, escort, activity restriction, complication, discharge, and follow-up modality remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
